top_signals.title: Disconnected Oil Futures Market Could See Price Spike within Weeks
top_signals.summary: For more than three months, oil market participants have hoped that the Middle East conflict would be resolved any day now, while about 13 million barrels per day (bpd) have been wiped off global supply due to the closed Strait of Hormuz. The oil futures market has been mostly guided by sentiment and traders’ hopes of an imminent peace deal – as U.S. President Donald Trump has been touting for weeks – with oil prices increasingly disconnected from the reality on the ground, or more precisely, in storage tanks. Disconnected The…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Every slice of China’s bond market has now succumbed to Japanisation
top_signals.summary: A scoop of belatedly spotting something and trying to make a thing of it
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Climate reporting is still a thing for US companies
top_signals.summary: Donald Trump may have dropped Biden-era rules but state law requires sustainability reporting
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Do art museums need ‘botanical curators’?
top_signals.summary: Rahel Kesselring, recently appointed at Switzerland’s Fondation Beyeler, is focused on how art can help us improve our sensitivity to the environment

top_signals.title: Why reviving US manufacturing is so hard
top_signals.summary: Skills shortages, shifting tariffs and complex permitting processes are major barriers

top_signals.title: Weekly mortgage demand surges nearly 11% higher, despite volatile interest rates
top_signals.summary: Mortgage demand from both current homeowners and potential buyers rose sharply, even  though interest rates rose as well.

top_signals.title: How pandemic car shortages are still making new and used cars expensive
top_signals.summary: Pandemic-era shortages are still pushing up used car prices, and automakers learned lessons from that time that stands to keep supply tight
